name: Et si on dansait!
classification_notes: Created in a 4-day workshop with design students, the typeface appears to be an experimental or expressive display face given its workshop context and exclamatory name suggesting movement and energy.
summary: A typeface born from a 4-day student workshop led by Corentin Noyer with DNMADE and DSAA students in Toulouse in February 2024.
description: Created during an intensive workshop at the Lycée Général et Technologique des Arènes in Toulouse, the typeface brought together 15 students from DNMADE and DSAA programmes over four days. The project sits within a pedagogical tradition of collaborative type creation, where process and experimentation are as much the point as the resulting letterforms.
foundry_blurb: Et si on dansait! emerged from a character creation workshop led with 15 students from DNMADE and DSAA programmes at the Lycée Général et Technologique des Arènes in Toulouse, completed over four days in February 2024.

name: Buro
classification_notes: The naming conventions and weight range suggest a workmanlike grotesque or neo-grotesque sans, typical of small independent French foundries producing functional text and display typefaces.
summary: A three-weight grotesque sans from independent French designer Corentin Noyer, available with matching italics for Regular and Medium.
description: Offered in Regular, Medium and Bold weights, with italic cuts for the Regular and Medium, Buro is a functional grotesque aimed at editorial and screen use. Sold individually or as a complete family, the licensing model covers print, screen and logotype applications. The foundry operates from France and sells directly through its own shop.
foundry_blurb: Buro is available as individual weights or as a complete family. Licences are sold by number of users and application type, covering print, screen and logotype use.

name: Safari
classification_notes: Safari is rooted in a photographic archive of wild, hand-painted and institutional signage collected from public spaces since 2014, placing it firmly in the display/vernacular category drawn from found lettering traditions.
summary: A typeface project rooted in a photographic archive of wild and institutional public signage collected across France from 2014 onwards.
description: Initiated as a personal research project in 2014, Safari draws on a corpus of photographs documenting hand-painted signs, road markings, promotional inscriptions and other public-space lettering. The collection deliberately sets institutional forms against vernacular, privately-made inscriptions to reveal the breadth of production methods, from colour and letterform drawing to materials and fixing techniques. It connects to François Chastanet's writing on the survival of written practices in public space.
foundry_blurb: Safari began in 2014 as a personal photographic research project focused on wild signage from public space, later expanding to cover all manner of public inscriptions including prohibitions, warnings, and decorative or promotional text. The deliberately heterogeneous corpus places institutional lettering such as road markings in tension with hand-painted vernacular inscriptions, highlighting the richness of means and modes of production across colour, letterform, layout, support, materials, marking techniques and fixing methods.

name: Corentin Noyer
classification_notes: The scraped page is a legal credits/mentions légales page for the Corentin Noyer website and contains no typeface product information whatsoever. No typeface name, category or design details could be extracted.
summary: No typeface information could be extracted from this page, which contains only legal and administrative credits for the Corentin Noyer website.
description: The URL provided leads to a French legal notices page listing site publisher details, hosting information, cookie policy and intellectual property terms for corentinoyer.fr. No specific typeface name, weights, design description or release year are present. A different URL pointing to an individual typeface product page would be needed to produce a valid entry.
foundry_blurb: Corentin Noyer is an independent French type designer based in Aspet, operating under SIRET 83163373000039. The site hosts his type catalogue alongside standard legal and data-protection disclosures required under French law.

name: EULA / Contrat de Licence Utilisateur Final
classification_notes: The scraped page is a licence agreement (EULA/CLUF) for Corentin Noyer's typefaces, not a typeface product page. No specific typeface information is present from which to derive category or subcategory.
summary: This page is a licence agreement for typefaces sold by Corentin Noyer, an independent French type designer, and contains no information about a specific typeface.
description: The URL provided resolves to Corentin Noyer's End User Licence Agreement (CLUF), detailing usage rights for print, screen and logotype applications across varying user counts. No individual typeface is described or featured on this page. To produce an accurate type directory entry, a product page for a specific typeface would be required.
foundry_blurb: Corentin Noyer is an independent French type designer and sole trader registered as an artist-author, offering typefaces for print, screen and logotype use under tiered licences covering one to fifty or more users. Custom modification and personalisation services are available for all typefaces in the catalogue.
